_comment: SC-001a / SC-002 baseline — manually captured, never auto-updated. Re-capture requires a reviewed commit.

reference_machine.cpu: x86_64 GitHub Actions hosted runner (ubuntu-latest, 4-core). See git log for exact capture commit.

lint_10kb._note: Values are Criterion 95% confidence-interval bounds. **2026-05-17 re-capture (PR #498).** Measured upper CI landed at 914µs on `ubuntu-latest` GHA runner (PR #498 CI run 25991729674), 3µs over the previous 911µs threshold — exactly the noise-band scenario the prior +10% widening was meant to absorb but which intervening complexity additions exceeded. Refreshed lower/mean/upper to the new capture (912 / 913 / 914); the new threshold (`upper_ci_us * 1.10` ≈ 1005µs) restores the +10% noise envelope. Drivers of the upward drift are intervening staging merges (PR 4b-B/C lattice landings, #259/#262/#280 decoder + parser work); PR 4b-D's hot-path flip is expected to retire the old PageContext machinery and bring this value back down, at which point a routine re-capture steps the baseline back down. **2026-05-05 widening (D8 amendment) — superseded by this re-capture.** The original 2026-04-26 baseline (lower 746 / mean 749 / upper 753) flapped on shared GHA `ubuntu-latest` runners (770–840µs across PR #301 / #302 / #303); per the D8 `decisions.md` amendment, the bench-runner owner approved a uniform +10% widening (746→821, 749→824, 753→828). The widening held until staging complexity additions exceeded the noise band, triggering this re-capture. The SC-001 16ms absolute target remains the load-bearing constitution gate; the re-capture is invisible to the SC-001 ceiling.
lint_10kb._p99_note: T098 / FR-030 / SC-008 tail-percentile gate, **partial activation**. Pins p99 cost of the `Arc<dyn Vocabulary<S>>::shape_admits` indirect call introduced in PR 2; vtable misses surface at the tail, not in the mean. **Status as of 2026-05-05:** only the absolute gate (`target_p99_us = 16000µs`, the SC-001 16ms constitutional ceiling applied to p99 specifically) is wired here. The drift gate (`p99_baseline * 1.05` per FR-030 / SC-008 `p99 ≤ baseline + 5%`) requires a `p99_us` baseline captured on the same hardware as the existing `upper_ci_us` baseline (`ubuntu-latest` per `reference_machine.profile`); a WSL2 dev-worktree capture would turn the drift gate into runner-noise flake on shared GHA runners. **Re-activation procedure (follow-up):** capture `p99_us` on `ubuntu-latest` via `scripts/capture-baselines.sh` (using `compute_percentiles_python` for capture-time / gate-time agreement), add the field to this block, and the drift check in `scripts/bench-check.sh::check_one_bench` will activate automatically (the gate is field-presence-gated). The absolute gate stays load-bearing across both states.

decoder_10kb_one_mangled_region._note: Engine::lint with `with_deep_scan()` on a 10KB document containing exactly one mangled portion (`(SERCET//NF)`) plus normal valid markings + prose. `SERCET` is the deliberate misspelling the SC-002 bench feeds the engine to force the decoder to fire — it is NOT a typo to be corrected. **2026-05-17 re-capture (PR #498).** Measured upper CI landed at 1158µs on `ubuntu-latest` GHA runner (PR #498 CI run 25991729674), 46µs over the previous 1113µs threshold — a substantive shift, not noise. Refreshed lower/mean/upper to the new capture (1155 / 1157 / 1158); the new threshold (`upper_ci_us * 1.10` ≈ 1274µs) restores headroom against intervening staging merges (PR 4b-B/C lattice landings, decoder priors work in #258/#259/#262, parser/recognizer additions). PR 4b-D's hot-path flip is expected to retire the old PageContext machinery on the decoder path and bring this value back down, at which point a routine re-capture steps the baseline back down. **2026-05-05 widening (D8 amendment) — superseded by this re-capture.** Original 2026-04-26 capture was lower 917 / mean 918 / upper 919; bumped uniformly +10% (917→1009, 918→1010, 919→1011) paired with the lint_10kb widening. The widening held until intervening complexity additions exceeded it. The SC-002 18ms absolute target remains the load-bearing constitution gate.

lint_scaling._note: SC-005 linear-scaling gate. R² is the coefficient of determination from an ordinary-least-squares fit of (input_size_bytes, mean_time_µs) across the input-size sweep in `crates/engine/benches/linear_scaling.rs` (currently 1KB → 100KB across seven points). 0.9 is the floor — substantially-better R² is normal for the strict path because per-document work is dominated by linear scanner + parser passes; a value below 0.9 usually indicates either super-linear behavior (a new quadratic somewhere) or pathological noise from the runner. Enforced by `scripts/bench-check.sh::check_linear_scaling`. Choice of input-size scaling vs. worker-count scaling is documented in the script header.

fix_throughput._note: Linear-scaling gate for Engine::fix FixMode::Apply. R² is the coefficient of determination from an OLS fit of (input_size_bytes, mean_time_µs) across the fix_throughput size sweep (1 MB → 100 MB, five points). 0.9 is the floor — mirroring the lint_scaling gate. A value below 0.9 indicates super-linear (e.g. quadratic) behavior in the apply path, which guards the Vec::splice-per-fix regression. Enforced by scripts/bench-check.sh::check_fix_throughput.

deadline_overhead._note: Spec 005 T018/T019. Compares `deadline_overhead_baseline` (Engine::lint, no deadline) against `deadline_overhead_with_deadline` (Engine::lint_with_options with a 1-hour deadline that never trips). The ratio is computed from Criterion's mean (middle CI value), not the upper-CI bound — clock jitter inflates CI width and would convert noise into false positives on a tight ratio gate. The gate is set at 10% (permissive starting point) rather than the 2% design target from `specs/005-engine-deadlines/spec.md` because the per-candidate Instant::now() cost varies dramatically by host: native Linux x86 vDSO clock_gettime is ~10–30ns and the spec's ≤2% target is reachable, but WSL2's virtualized HV clock is ~500–700ns and observed end-to-end overhead is 7–12% on the same fixture. Tightening this gate to 2% is a follow-up once a CI-runner baseline is captured (mirrors the `MARQUE_BENCH_SKIP_REGRESSION` pattern for the +10% absolute regression gate). Enforced by `scripts/bench-check.sh::check_deadline_overhead`.

fix_10kb_pass2_only._note: PR 7c (T085) — SC-001 absolute target for the two-pass fix pipeline's pass-2-only path. Exercises the no-reshape short-circuit: pass-1 produces zero applied fixes, the pre-pass-1 cache stays empty, the re-parse arm short-circuits, and pass-2 dispatches against the post-pass-0 buffer. No `upper_ci_us` baseline captured here — capture and add to enable the +10% regression gate; the absolute SC-001 16ms ceiling stays load-bearing in the meantime. Capture procedure: run `cargo bench --bench fix_10kb -- --save-baseline pr7c` on this branch and on `origin/staging` (pre-pr7c), then add the `upper_ci_us` / `p99_us` fields. FR-033 delta gate (p99 ≤ baseline × 1.05) activates automatically once `p99_us` lands. See `crates/engine/benches/fix_10kb.rs`.

fix_10kb_two_pass._note: PR 7c (T085) — SC-001 absolute target for the two-pass fix pipeline's full path. Exercises pass-0 corrections, pass-1 Localized fixes (C001 typo correction), the pre-pass-1 attrs cache population, post-pass-1 re-lint via `lint_with_options_internal_with_cache`, FR-023 disambiguation, and I-18 overlap demotion. (The originally-planned `PrecedingFixPenalty` application at the pass-2 threshold gate was retired in this same PR per D-7.22; the bench no longer exercises any penalty-application codepath.) As with `fix_10kb_pass2_only`, no `upper_ci_us` baseline is captured here — operator runs `cargo bench --bench fix_10kb -- --save-baseline pr7c` to populate. The SC-001 16ms absolute target is the load-bearing gate. See `crates/engine/benches/fix_10kb.rs`.
